description: A wide (1200x675) designed campaign banner for DHS's 'Know2Protect' initiative against online child exploitation. The background is a deep blue gradient with faint diagonal brush streaks and translucent circular bokeh/bubble shapes. On the left, a photographic image of a young girl in a gray hoodie sits cross-legged, head down, illuminated by the glow of a black tablet she is holding/looking at; the dramatic low lighting evokes the isolation of a child alone online at night. Floating near her are two small colored chat-icon graphics: an orange speech bubble with a white heart and a magenta speech bubble with a smiley face, suggesting incoming social-media messages. On the right side are the white U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal and the large white 'know2protect' logo (speech bubble with three dots), with the trademark symbol and the tagline 'Together We Can Stop Online Child Exploitation'. Below that, white text reads 'To learn more, go to know2protect.gov'. Style: polished, somber-but-hopeful PSA branding combining stock photography with flat graphic overlays. Intent: drive parents and the public to the Know2Protect website; raise awareness of online predatory contact with children.
notable_text: know2protect — Together We Can Stop Online Child Exploitation; To learn more, go to know2protect.gov
